status_detail: Commissioned in May 2022 and fully operational since Q1 2023. One of Europe's first large-scale industrial green hydrogen plants. Supplies Fertiberia ammonia plant on-site. Part of Iberdrola's broader IPCEI hydrogen program targeting 800 MW by 2027. Plant uses 100 MW solar PV with 5 MW/20 MWh battery storage. 16 PEM stacks of 1.25 MW each from Nel Hydrogen Electrolyser. 11 hydrogen storage tanks at 60 bar.

data_management.narrative_summary: The Puertollano Green Hydrogen Plant is a 20 MW PEM electrolysis facility in Puertollano, Ciudad Real, Spain, developed by Iberdrola in partnership with Fertiberia. It was one of Europe's first large-scale industrial green hydrogen plants when commissioned in May 2022. The plant uses 16 Nel Hydrogen PEM electrolyzer stacks of 1.25 MW each, powered by an adjacent 100 MW solar PV plant with a 5 MW/20 MWh lithium-ion battery storage system. It produces approximately 3,000 tonnes of green hydrogen per year, which is used on-site by Fertiberia's ammonia plant (capacity >200,000 tonnes/year) to produce green ammonia and emission-free fertilizers, replacing hydrogen previously produced via steam methane reforming. The plant includes 11 hydrogen storage tanks at 60 bar with total storage capacity exceeding 6.5 tonnes. The total investment was approximately €150 million and the project is part of Iberdrola's broader IPCEI program targeting 800 MW of green hydrogen capacity by 2027.

power.grid_connection_detail: 100 MW solar PV plant co-located on-site with 5 MW / 20 MWh lithium-ion battery storage system

rag_metadata.text_for_embedding: Puertollano Green Hydrogen Plant is a 20 MW PEM electrolysis facility in Puertollano, Ciudad Real, Spain, developed by Iberdrola with Fertiberia. Uses 16 Nel Hydrogen PEM stacks of 1.25 MW each, powered by 100 MW solar PV with 5 MW battery storage. Produces ~3,000 t/yr green hydrogen for on-site Fertiberia ammonia plant to produce green ammonia and emission-free fertilizers. 11 H₂ storage tanks at 60 bar. €150M investment. Commissioned May 2022. One of Europe's first large-scale industrial green hydrogen plants. Operational. Spain. Europe. ammonia. PEM. 20MW. Solar PV. First-of-a-kind. Operational since Q1 2023.
